evidence: 11 packages with clear role separation: api/, api/model/, config/, exploration/, exploration/spi/, indexer/blacklab/, model/, model/exploration/, model/sketch/, query/, utils/
exploration/spi/ contains exactly one file (ExplorationService.java plus package-info.java) — the SPI pattern is valid but adds navigational overhead at this scale

Three parallel representations of the same 'graph edge' concept: model/exploration/Edge (domain), api/model/ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry (nested record), api/model/ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry (separate nested record) — the domain type is never reused at the API layer
model/exploration/ has 14 files (514 LOC) and is referenced by both exploration/ and api/model/ — density is acceptable given each type is small and focused
viz/RadialPlot.java has no package-info, unlike all other packages
package_organization.impact_scope: Low — navigability is generally strong; the duplication and spi-overhead are modest friction
package_organization.fix_scope: Small — merge ExplorationException into spi/, consolidate EdgeEntry types or reuse model/exploration/Edge in assembler

evidence: ExploreResponse sealed interface declares @JsonProperty("discovered_nouns") on discoveredNouns() at line 45 — SingleSeed records it correctly, but MultiSeed records the physical field as sourceSeeds with @JsonProperty("source_seeds") and the override method is a no-Jackson-serialized delegate. The annotation on the interface method is effectively a documented lie for MultiSeed callers reading the interface

ExplorationResult.of() factory Javadoc says all parameters 'must not be null' for 5 collections, but only seeds gets runtime enforcement via List.copyOf; the remaining 4 parameters (seedCollocates, seedCollocateFrequencies, discoveredNouns, coreCollocates) have no null guards — a contradiction between stated and enforced contract

ExplorationService interface Javadoc says '@throws IllegalArgumentException if seeds is empty or fewer than 2', but the implementation (SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreMultiSeed) also throws ExplorationException which is not mentioned in the interface contract

BlackLabQueryExecutor test constructor Javadoc says 'Callers must not invoke any method that delegates to blackLabIndex or collocateQueryHelper when those are null' — a caller contract that the compiler cannot enforce and tests could accidentally violate
RelationPatternUtils.buildFullPattern(config, headword) Javadoc says 'returned unchanged if headword is null/blank' which the code honours — a positive contract consistency
contract_coherence.impact_scope: Medium — the @JsonProperty annotation discrepancy on the ExploreResponse interface could mislead API consumers or developers adding new variants; the incomplete null validation in ExplorationResult.of() could produce NullPointerException in runtime
contract_coherence.fix_scope: Small — move @JsonProperty annotation off the interface to a comment; add Objects.requireNonNull guards in ExplorationResult.of() for remaining params

evidence: ExplorationResult Javadoc says 'Immutable result' and 'All collections are non-null' but the private constructor only applies List.copyOf to seeds; the other 5 fields (seedCollocates, seedCollocateFrequencies, discoveredNouns, coreCollocates, perSeedCollocates) are stored by reference — callers retaining the original collections can mutate them post-construction. ComparisonResult correctly uses List.copyOf for both of its fields, making the inconsistency visible by contrast
DiscoveredNoun.sharedCollocateList() creates a new ArrayList<>(sharedCollocates.keySet()) on every invocation with no caching — the record is used in iteration-heavy exploration paths
MultiSeedExplorer.identifyCommonCollocates correctly caps threshold to seedCount with Math.min(minShared, seedCount) — prevents degenerate case where minShared > seeds

BlackLabQueryExecutor test constructor uses an anonymous subclass 'new CollocateQueryHelper((BlackLabIndex) null) {}' to avoid real construction when index is null — the intent is documented but the pattern is unusual and fragile if CollocateQueryHelper gains non-overridable init logic
Control flow in SemanticFieldExplorer.fetchExamples deduplication loop is clear and correct; early-exit on maxExamples is properly placed
logic_clarity.impact_scope: Medium for ExplorationResult mutability — post-construction mutation of collections can produce invisible bugs in multi-threaded or cached usage; low for the rest
logic_clarity.fix_scope: Small — apply List.copyOf/Map.copyOf to all 5 remaining ExplorationResult constructor fields; cache sharedCollocateList() or document the per-call cost

evidence: @NonNull/@Nullable JSpecify annotations are applied consistently on all QueryExecutor and CollocateQueryPort method signatures, ExplorationService, and RelationPatternUtils helpers — positive signal

@SuppressWarnings("NullAway") on BlackLabQueryExecutor(BlackLabIndex) test constructor (line ~57) suppresses null-safety analysis for a constructor that has a documented precondition — removes compile-time enforcement

ExplorationResult.of() static factory declares 5 @NonNull-documented Map/List parameters but provides no Objects.requireNonNull guards for 4 of them; List.copyOf(seeds) provides implicit null enforcement only for seeds
ComparisonResult.specificTo(String noun) returns a mutable List via Collectors.toList() — inconsistent with the broader pattern of returning immutable views; callers could mutate the returned list
HttpApiUtils.sendJsonResponse(exchange, Object body) loses static type information at the API boundary — inherent to the generic JSON serializer, low severity
CollocateProfile.nounScores() returns Map<String, Double> with no nullability annotation on the return type — minor gap in otherwise well-annotated domain model
type_safety.impact_scope: Low-to-medium — suppressed NullAway and incomplete factory guards are the highest-risk items; the mutable return from specificTo() is a correctness risk if callers mutate the list
type_safety.fix_scope: Small — add null guards in ExplorationResult.of(), remove suppress and refactor test constructor, change specificTo() to return List.copyOf(result)

evidence: ExplorationException lives in pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.exploration (implementation package) but is imported and caught directly by api/HttpApiUtils.java — this punches a hole in the exploration/spi/ abstraction boundary that ExplorationHandlers and WordSketchApiServer otherwise respect. Moving ExplorationException to exploration/spi/ or model/ would close the leak

Three parallel edge types with no reuse: model/exploration/Edge (domain record), ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry (nested record with logDice field), ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry (nested record with weight field instead of logDice) — the domain type is converted-away in ExploreResponseAssembler rather than being used directly; the field name divergence (logDice vs weight) is unexplained
Main.java imports pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.exploration.SemanticFieldExplorer directly (bypassing the spi/ interface) — acceptable for the composition root, not a boundary violation per se
No import cycles detected: api/ → exploration/spi/ → (none), exploration/ → query/ → model/sketch/, config/ → model/ — the DAG is clean
ExplorationHandlers correctly declares ExplorationService (spi type) not SemanticFieldExplorer (impl type) — boundary is respected for the primary consumer
cross_module_architecture.impact_scope: Medium — the ExplorationException boundary leak means api/ is not fully isolated from exploration/ internals; the three-way edge duplication increases maintenance surface when edge semantics change
cross_module_architecture.fix_scope: Small-to-medium — move ExplorationException to spi/ package; evaluate whether ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry and ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry can share a common type or reuse model/exploration/Edge

issues.summary: @JsonProperty("discovered_nouns") on ExploreResponse.discoveredNouns() interface method is inaccurate for the MultiSeed variant
evidence: Interface method at line 45: @JsonProperty("discovered_nouns") List<DiscoveredNounEntry> discoveredNouns()
MultiSeed record field at line 131: @JsonProperty("source_seeds") List<DiscoveredNounEntry> sourceSeeds
MultiSeed.discoveredNouns() override at line 140 is a plain Java method delegate — not a Jackson-serialized property; Jackson serializes sourceSeeds as "source_seeds", not "discovered_nouns"
Developers reading the interface contract expect discoveredNouns() to always map to JSON key "discovered_nouns"; for MultiSeed it maps to "source_seeds"
issues.suggestion: Remove @JsonProperty("discovered_nouns") from the interface method and replace it with a Javadoc note explaining the two JSON key conventions. Keep the concrete annotations on SingleSeed and MultiSeed records where they actually govern serialization.
issues.impact_scope: Low — the JSON output is correct; the risk is developer confusion when extending the sealed interface

issues.summary: ExplorationResult.of() factory documents all parameters as non-null but enforces it for only one
evidence: Private constructor line 43: this.seeds = List.copyOf(seeds) — enforces non-null implicitly
Lines 44-48: seedCollocates, seedCollocateFrequencies, discoveredNouns, coreCollocates, perSeedCollocates are all stored directly without any null check
Static factory Javadoc says 'must not be null' for all six parameters
Class Javadoc says 'All collections are non-null' — a guarantee the constructor does not uphold for five of the six fields
issues.suggestion: Add Objects.requireNonNull(...) for seedCollocates, seedCollocateFrequencies, discoveredNouns, coreCollocates, and perSeedCollocates in the private constructor, matching the 'must not be null' guarantee in the Javadoc.
issues.impact_scope: Low — callers are internal and unlikely to pass null today; the risk is future callers relying on the documented contract

issues.summary: ExplorationResult claims to be immutable but only defensively copies the seeds field
evidence: Class Javadoc: 'Immutable result of semantic field exploration'
Private constructor line 43: this.seeds = List.copyOf(seeds) — only seeds is defensively copied
Lines 44-48: seedCollocates, seedCollocateFrequencies, discoveredNouns, coreCollocates, perSeedCollocates stored by direct reference — caller-retained references can mutate the stored state
ComparisonResult (same package, same pattern): both nouns and collocates are List.copyOf'd — making the inconsistency visible by contrast
issues.suggestion: Wrap the remaining five fields with Map.copyOf / List.copyOf in the private constructor, or replace mutable Map types with Map.of / Map.copyOf at the call sites in MultiSeedExplorer and SingleSeedExplorer. Update the Javadoc if full immutability cannot be achieved (e.g. when nested Maps contain mutable values).
issues.impact_scope: Medium — the risk is subtle state corruption in concurrent or cached usage; current single-threaded call sites are safe but the class contract advertises a safety guarantee it does not deliver

issues.summary: @SuppressWarnings("NullAway") on BlackLabQueryExecutor test constructor removes null-safety enforcement for a constructor with a documented null precondition
evidence: @SuppressWarnings("NullAway") annotation on the package-private constructor accepting BlackLabIndex
The constructor body assigns this.blackLabIndex = index without a null check; the Javadoc says 'Callers must not invoke any method that delegates to blackLabIndex when null'
The collocateQueryHelper initialization uses an anonymous subclass 'new CollocateQueryHelper((BlackLabIndex) null) {}' — NullAway would flag the null cast without the suppression
issues.suggestion: Extract the test-only null-index path to a static factory method (e.g. BlackLabQueryExecutor.forTesting()) that clearly documents its constraints, allowing the primary constructor to remain fully null-safe. Remove the suppress annotation.
issues.impact_scope: Low — affects test code paths only; production construction always passes a non-null index

issues.summary: ExplorationException lives in the exploration/ implementation package but is imported directly by api/HttpApiUtils, bypassing the exploration/spi/ boundary
evidence: HttpApiUtils.java line 5: import pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.exploration.ExplorationException
ExplorationHandlers and WordSketchApiServer both correctly import only pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.exploration.spi.ExplorationService
ExplorationException.java is in the exploration/ package alongside implementation classes (SemanticFieldExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, etc.) — not in spi/
The SPI pattern advertised by exploration/spi/ is intended to decouple api/ from implementation details; the HttpApiUtils import defeats this for the exception type
issues.suggestion: Move ExplorationException into exploration/spi/ (or model/exploration/) so that the api/ layer's only direct dependency on exploration/ internals is through the spi/ boundary. This matches the stated design intent of the spi/ package.
issues.impact_scope: Low-to-medium — no functional impact today; the boundary leak matters if the exploration/ implementation is ever replaced or modularised

issues.summary: Three parallel edge representations with no shared base: model/exploration/Edge, ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry, and ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry
evidence: model/exploration/Edge: record(String source, String target, double weight, RelationEdgeType type)
ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry: record(String source, String target, @JsonProperty("log_dice") double logDice, RelationEdgeType type)
ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry: record(String source, String target, double weight, RelationEdgeType type)
ExploreResponseAssembler builds List<Edge> internally then converts to EdgeEntry — the intermediate domain type adds a conversion step with no reuse at the API layer
ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry and model/exploration/Edge have the same field names (weight) but are separate types
issues.suggestion: Either reuse model/exploration/Edge directly in both response records (adding @JsonProperty("log_dice") via a mixin or renaming the field), or collapse ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry and ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry into a single shared response type in api/model/. The current three-type arrangement means edge serialization logic changes must be made in three places.
issues.impact_scope: Low — duplication is contained; the main cost is maintenance friction when edge fields change
